---
title: "Sigmoid Commercial Intelligence Lab"
subtitle: "Turning commercial strategy into evidence-based, versioned, testable intelligence"
toc: false
---

## What this project is

The **Sigmoid Commercial Intelligence Lab** is a reproducible research and decision-intelligence
project. It takes the Sigmoid Analytica investor/operator proposal — which positions the CRM as the
foundation for AI readiness — and converts it into a *living system* where commercial strategy can be
questioned, measured, tested, and revised as evidence accumulates.

It is not a rewrite of the proposal. It is the machinery for deciding whether the proposal's claims
are true, and under what conditions.

> **Status: v0.1 — hypothesis stage.**
> No recommendation on this site is validated. Every figure, score, and ranking is *illustrative*
> until it has passed through the evidence and validation stages of the CIS decision lifecycle.

## Why the PDF is treated as Hypothesis v0.1

The source proposal is a strong articulation of a commercial thesis. But a proposal is, by
definition, an argument made *before* the evidence is in. Treating it as settled truth would bake in
untested assumptions about which market to target, how to position the CRM, and what customers will
pay.

So we treat the PDF as **Hypothesis v0.1**: a structured set of falsifiable claims. Each claim is
extracted into the evidence register, given an operational definition, and scheduled for validation.
The proposal earns its authority by surviving scrutiny — not by being the first document written.

## What CIS is

The project is governed by the **Commercial Intelligence Specification (CIS)** — a specification that
treats commercial strategy as a *computational decision system*. Under CIS, no recommendation is
legitimate unless it can trace its lineage through a fixed lifecycle:

```text
Question → Hypothesis → Operational Definition → Evidence Collection → Analysis
        → Model → Validation → Recommendation → Implementation → Monitoring → Learning
```

Every artifact in this repository — a score, a framework, a dashboard, a chapter of the working
paper — is expected to name where it sits in that lifecycle. See the full
[Commercial Intelligence Specification](cis/commercial-intelligence-specification.md).

## How strategy becomes testable intelligence

| Traditional strategy deck | Commercial Intelligence Lab |
|---|---|
| Claims asserted once | Claims registered, dated, and scored for evidence quality |
| Numbers chosen to persuade | Numbers computed by transparent, versioned functions |
| Conclusions fixed at print time | Conclusions recomputed as evidence changes |
| No way to check the reasoning | Scoring logic under automated test (`pytest`) |
| Positioning by intuition | Positioning by explicit, weighted criteria |

Concretely, the lab provides: transparent [scoring frameworks](frameworks/index.qmd) with editable
weights, [interactive dashboards](dashboards/market-prioritisation.qmd) that recompute on the sample
data, an [evidence register](cis/commercial-intelligence-specification.md) that separates hypothesis
from fact, a [working paper](working-paper/index.qmd) that structures each open question, and a test
suite that guards the integrity of every calculation.

## The first three decision questions

CIS is driven by questions, not conclusions. The lab opens with three:

1. **Market entry** — *Which market segment should Sigmoid target first for the CRM?*
   Explored in the [Market Prioritisation dashboard](dashboards/market-prioritisation.qmd) and
   [Working Paper Ch.5](working-paper/05-market-prioritisation.qmd).

2. **Positioning** — *How should the CRM be positioned: as a CRM, a Revenue Operations Platform, or
   an AI Readiness Platform?* Explored in the
   [Positioning dashboard](dashboards/positioning-dashboard.qmd) and
   [Working Paper Ch.4](working-paper/04-crm-positioning.qmd).

3. **Evidence threshold** — *What evidence is required before committing to a go-to-market strategy?*
   Explored in [Working Paper Ch.7](working-paper/07-go-to-market.qmd) and
   [Ch.8](working-paper/08-validation-and-tests.qmd).

These are deliberately unanswered here. The purpose of the lab is to answer them *with evidence*, in
public, in a way anyone can re-run.

## Where to start

- **New collaborators:** read the [README](README.md) for setup, then the
  [CIS](cis/commercial-intelligence-specification.md).
- **Reviewers of the thesis:** start with the [Working Paper](working-paper/index.qmd).
- **Analysts:** open the [dashboards](dashboards/market-prioritisation.qmd) and the
  [frameworks](frameworks/index.qmd).
- **Architects:** read the [Decision Records](docs/decision-records/index.qmd).
